_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/ DNB/ PG Diploma/ equivalent (in Psychiatry)
clinical_indications: Diagnosis of depression (symptoms must present for at least 2 weeks)
Mild depression with no improvement after 4 to 6 weeks of non-pharmacological treatment (consider pharmacotherapy)
Moderate/severe depression warranting pharmacological intervention (e.g., Escitalopram, Fluoxetine)

Referral to secondary care for difficulty in diagnosis, no improvement with first-line medications, special populations (elderly, pregnancy, lactation, children, adolescents), comorbid medical illness/substance use, or suicidal risk assessment

Referral to tertiary care for no improvement in 2nd line treatment, immediate risk for suicidal attempt/thought, needing intense counselling/psychotherapy, or co-morbid substance (cannabis/poly substance)

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailed history and chronicity
Admission document signed by empaneled psychiatrist

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment and need of hospitalization
Was the admission document signed by an empanelled psychiatrist?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed treatment notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Admission document not signed by an empaneled psychiatrist
Clinical notes missing detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment or need of hospitalization
One or more mandatory investigations (Complete hemogram, Thyroid function test, Serum sodium, Resting ECG) not submitted
Detailed Discharge Summary not submitted or missing date of follow-up

additional_information.special_empanelment: As per the provisions of the Mental Health Act 2017
clinical_key_pointers: The provisions under Mental Healthcare Act 2017 be referred for details on Admission & Discharge criteria.
surgical_options: Electro Convulsive Therapy (ECT) for Catatonia/Suicidality (at Tertiary Care, with a high threshold for invasive procedures)
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Rule out associated comorbid medical conditions (Diabetes, Stroke, Epilepsy, Cancer, Coronary Artery Disease, Auto Immune disorder); Assess for other medical comorbidities; Assess other psychiatric comorbidities.
additional_information.referral_criteria:
To Secondary Care: Difficulty in making diagnosis; No improvement after 4 to 6 weeks of treatment with first line medications; Depression in special population (Elderly/ Pregnancy/ Lactation/ Children/ Adolescents); Comorbid medical illness/ Substance use; Suicidal risk assessment.
To Tertiary Care: No improvement in 2nd line treatment; Immediate risk for suicidal attempt/thought; Needing intense counselling/psychotherapy; Co Morbid Substance (Cannabis/ Poly substance).
contraindications: Rule out secondary medical cause of depression (e.g., Hypothyroidism)
Rule out use of anticancer drugs (Cyclophosphamide), anti retroviral drugs (Efavirenz, Zidovudine), Antibiotics (Dapsone, Ethambutol), Anabolic Steroids, Propanolol
Rule out Bipolar Disorder/Grief/ Adjustment Disorder
